questions.prompt: What's the difference between a True deny (red) and a Simulated deny (green) in the Unified Audit?
choices: True denies happen on Windows; Simulated denies happen on Mac
True denies actually blocked; Simulated denies show what would have blocked if the computer was Secured
True denies are recent; Simulated denies are historical
True denies require admin; Simulated denies don't
questions.explanation: Simulated denies are the most useful artefact of Learning / Monitor Only. They tell you what your future Secured posture would have blocked, without anyone actually getting blocked yet.

questions.prompt: The Remove White Noise filter should usually be on when you're scanning the Unified Audit for relevant events.
questions.explanation: Without the noise filter, the audit fills with system-DLL denies and the signal you're looking for becomes hard to find. The noise filter is documented as filtering 'well-known white noise denies'.
